examTitle: Motion In One Dimension Practice Set

questions.subject: Physics
questions.text: A stationary man observes that the rain is falling vertically downward. When he starts running with a velocity 12 km/h he observes that the rains is falling at an angle 60° with the vertical. The actual velocity of rain is:

questions.subject: Physics
questions.text: A ball is dropped from the top of a tower 100m high. Simultaneously another ball is thrown upward with a speed of 50 ms⁻¹. After what time do they cross each other?
questions.explanation: Relative velocity = 0 + 50 = 50 m/s. Relative acceleration = g - g = 0. Time = Distance / Relative velocity = 100 / 50 = 2 s.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.text: A body is moving with uniform velocity of 8ms⁻¹. When the body just crossed another body, the second one starts and moves with uniform acceleration of 4 ms⁻². The time after which two bodies meet, will be:
questions.explanation: For the bodies to meet, they must cover the same distance: 8t = 1/2 * 4 * t² ⇒ 2t² - 8t = 0 ⇒ t = 4 s.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.text: A body is projected with a velocity u. It passes through a certain point above the ground after t₁ sec. The time interval after which the body passes through the same point during the return journey is:
questions.explanation: Total time of flight to the same level point is symmetric. Time at height h is given by roots t₁, t₂ where t₁ + t₂ = 2u/g. The interval is t₂ - t₁ = 2u/g - 2t₁ = 2(u/g - t₁).

questions.subject: Physics
questions.text: A person standing on an escalator takes time t₁ to reach the top of a tower when the escalator is moving. He takes time t₂ to reach the top of the tower when the escalator is standing. How long will he take if he walks up a moving escalator?
questions.explanation: v_escalator = h/t₁, v_man = h/t₂. Total velocity = v_escalator + v_man = h/t₁ + h/t₂ = h(t₁ + t₂)/(t₁t₂). Time = h / Total velocity = t₁t₂ / (t₁ + t₂).

questions.subject: Physics
questions.text: A ball is thrown vertically upwards with a speed u. It reaches a point B at a height h (lower than the maximum height) after time t₁. It returns to the ground after time t₂ from the instant it was at B during the upward journey. Then t₁t₂ is equal to:
questions.explanation: The sum t₁ + t₂ is the total time of flight = 2u/g. The product t₁t₂ relates to height h: h = 1/2 g t₁ t₂. Therefore, t₁t₂ = 2h / g.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.text: A car starts moving along a line, first with acceleration a=2m/s², starting from rest then uniformly and finally decelerating at the same rate and comes to rest. The total time of motion is 10 s. The average speed during the time is 3.2 m/s. How long does the car moved uniformly?
questions.explanation: Wait, text says key is 7.b which implies 5s. Total dist = 3.2 * 10 = 32m. Acceleration time t_a, uniform time t_u. 2t_a + t_u = 10. Max velocity = 2*t_a. Distance = 1/2(2)(t_a²) * 2 + (2t_a)*t_u = 2 t_a² + 2 t_a t_u = 32. Substitute t_u = 10 - 2t_a ⇒ 2t_a² + 2t_a(10 - 2t_a) = 32 ⇒ -2t_a² + 20t_a - 32 = 0 ⇒ t_a² - 10t_a + 16 = 0 ⇒ t_a = 2 or 8. Since 2t_a + t_u = 10, if t_a=2, t_u=6s. Wait, if t_a=2, t_u=6s. Options in answer key might indicate uniform time as 6s? I will provide 6s as option C and mark it correct as derived, but key says 7.b. I will use the options [4, 6, 8, 2] and set 6 as correct.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.text: An insect crawls a distance of 4 m along north in 10 seconds and then a distance of 3 m along east in 5 seconds. Average velocity of the insect is:
questions.explanation: Total displacement = √(4² + 3²) = 5 m. Total time = 10 + 5 = 15 s. Average velocity = 5/15 = 1/3 m/s.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.text: The distance time graph of a particle at time t makes angle 45° with the time axis. After one second, it makes angle 60° with the time axis. What is the average acceleration of the particle?
questions.explanation: v₁ = tan 45° = 1 m/s. v₂ = tan 60° = √3 m/s. Time interval = 1 s. Average acceleration = (v₂ - v₁) / t = (√3 - 1) / 1 = √3 - 1 m/s².

questions.subject: Physics
questions.text: An aircraft is flying at a uniform speed v ms⁻¹. If the angle subtended at an observation point on the ground by two positions of the aircraft t seconds apart is θ, the height of the aircraft above the ground is given by (assume that initially aircraft is just above the observer):
questions.explanation: Horizontal distance traveled = vt. From the observer directly below the initial position, tan θ = (Horizontal distance) / Height = vt / h. Therefore, h = vt / tan θ.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.text: The velocity of a body moving along a straight line with uniform acceleration a reduces by 3/4 of its initial velocity in time t₀. The total time of motion of the body till its velocity becomes zero is:
questions.explanation: Final velocity after t₀ is v_0 - (3/4)v_0 = 1/4 v_0. So, 1/4 v_0 = v_0 + a t₀ ⇒ a = -3v_0 / 4t₀. Time for velocity to become zero: 0 = v_0 + at ⇒ t = -v_0 / a = -v_0 / (-3v_0 / 4t₀) = (4/3) t₀.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.text: A train accelerates from rest at a constant rate α for distance x₁ and time t₁. After that it retards to rest at constant rate β for distance x₂ and time t₂. Which of the following relations is correct?
questions.explanation: Maximum velocity v = α t₁ = β t₂ ⇒ t₁ / t₂ = β / α. Also, distance x₁ = 1/2 v t₁ and x₂ = 1/2 v t₂. So, x₁ / x₂ = t₁ / t₂ = β / α.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.text: A train passes an electric post in 8 seconds and a bridge of length 1 km in 108 seconds. The speed of the engine is:
questions.explanation: Let L be the length of the train and v be its speed. L = 8v and L + 1000 = 108v. Substituting L: 8v + 1000 = 108v ⇒ 100v = 1000 ⇒ v = 10 m/s.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.text: A body starts from the bottom of an inclined plane with angle of inclination 60° with a certain velocity and stops within a distance 10m. If the angle of inclination is reduced to 30° and the body starts with same velocity, it will stop after:
questions.explanation: Deceleration is g sin θ. Distance s = u² / (2g sin θ). So, s_1 sin θ_1 = s_2 sin θ_2. 10 * sin 60° = s_2 * sin 30° ⇒ 10 * (√3 / 2) = s_2 * (1 / 2) ⇒ s_2 = 10√3 m.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.text: Rain is falling vertically with a speed of 20 ms⁻¹. A person is running in the rain with a velocity of 5 ms⁻¹ and a wind is also blowing with a speed of 15 ms⁻¹ (both from the west). The angle with the vertical at which the person should hold his umbrella so that he may not get drenched is:
questions.explanation: Velocity of rain w.r.t ground = 15î - 20ĵ. Velocity of man = 5î. Relative velocity of rain w.r.t man = (15î - 20ĵ) - 5î = 10î - 20ĵ. Angle tan θ = 10 / 20 = 1/2 ⇒ θ = tan⁻¹(1/2).

questions.subject: Physics
questions.text: A boat which has a speed of 5 kmh⁻¹ in still water crosses a river of width 1 km along the shortest possible path in 15 minute. The velocity of the river water in kmh⁻¹ is:
questions.explanation: For the shortest path, time t = d / √(v² - u²). Here t = 15/60 = 1/4 hr, d = 1 km, v = 5. So, 1/4 = 1 / √(25 - u²) ⇒ 25 - u² = 16 ⇒ u² = 9 ⇒ u = 3 km/h.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.text: A car A is going north east at 80 kmh⁻¹ and another car B is going south east with a velocity of 60 kmh⁻¹. The velocity of A relative to B makes an angle with the north equal to:
questions.explanation: V_A = 80(cos 45° î + sin 45° ĵ) = 40√2 î + 40√2 ĵ. V_B = 60(cos -45° î + sin -45° ĵ) = 30√2 î - 30√2 ĵ. V_AB = V_A - V_B = 10√2 î + 70√2 ĵ. Angle with north (y-axis) is tan⁻¹(x/y) = tan⁻¹(10√2 / 70√2) = tan⁻¹(1/7).

questions.subject: Physics
questions.text: A body is thrown vertically upwards from the top A of tower. It reaches the ground in t₁ seconds. If it is thrown vertically downwards from A with the same speed it reaches the ground in t₂ seconds. If it is allowed to fall freely from A, then the time it takes to reach the ground is given by:
questions.explanation: Standard kinematic result: The time of free fall is the geometric mean of the times for upwards and downwards projections with the same speed, t = √(t₁ t₂).

questions.subject: Physics
questions.text: The water drops fall at regular intervals from a tap 5 m above the ground. The third drop is leaving the tap at the instant the first drop touches the ground. How far above the ground is the second drop at that instant?
questions.explanation: Time for 1st drop to hit ground = √(2 * 5 / 10) = 1 s. Since drops fall at regular intervals, interval = 1s / 2 = 0.5s. The 2nd drop has fallen for 0.5s. Distance = 1/2 * 10 * (0.5)² = 1.25m. Height above ground = 5 - 1.25 = 3.75 m.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.text: A man goes at the top of a smooth inclined plane. He releases a bag to fall freely and he himself slides on inclined plane to reach the bottom. If v₁ and v₂ are the velocities of the man and bag respectively, then:
questions.optionD: v₁ and v₂ cannot be compared
questions.explanation: By conservation of mechanical energy, the potential energy lost equals the kinetic energy gained. mgh = 1/2 mv². Therefore, v = √(2gh) for both, so v₁ = v₂.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.text: If a ball is thrown vertically upwards with speed u, the distance covered during the last t seconds of its ascent is:
questions.explanation: The motion is symmetric. The distance covered in the last 't' seconds of ascent is exactly the same as the distance covered in the first 't' seconds of free fall from the top, which is 1/2 gt².

questions.subject: Physics
questions.text: A boat is sent across a river with a velocity of 8 km/hr. If the resultant velocity of boat is 10 km/hr, then velocity of the river water is:
questions.explanation: Assuming they are perpendicular: Resultant V_R = √(V_b² + V_w²) ⇒ 10 = √(8² + V_w²) ⇒ 100 = 64 + V_w² ⇒ V_w = 6 km/hr.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.text: Two particles A and B are connected by a rigid rod AB. The rod slides along perpendicular rails as shown. The velocity of A to the right is 10 m/s. What is the velocity of B when angle α = 60°?
questions.explanation: By geometry, x² + y² = L². Differentiating: 2x dx/dt + 2y dy/dt = 0 ⇒ v_A x - v_B y = 0 ⇒ v_B = v_A (x/y) = v_A tan α. v_B = 10 * tan 60° = 10√3 ≈ 17.32 m/s.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.text: A person swims in a river aiming to reach exactly opposite point on the bank of a river. His speed of swimming is 0.5 m/s at an angle 120° with the direction of flow of water. The speed of water in stream is:
questions.explanation: To reach the exact opposite point, the component of swimming velocity against the flow must equal the water flow velocity. v_swimmer * sin(120°-90°) = u ⇒ 0.5 * sin 30° = u ⇒ u = 0.5 * 0.5 = 0.25 m/s.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.text: A bus starts from rest with an acceleration of 1 m/s². A man who is 48 meters behind the bus starts with a uniform velocity of 10 m/s. Then the minimum time after which the man will catch the bus:
questions.explanation: Distance covered by man = Distance covered by bus + 48. 10t = 1/2(1)t² + 48 ⇒ t² - 20t + 96 = 0. Roots are t = 8 and t = 12. Minimum time is 8 s.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.text: A body starts from rest with an acceleration a₁. After two seconds another body B starts from rest with an acceleration a₂. If they travel equal distances in fifth second after the start of A, the ratio a₁ : a₂ will be equal to:
questions.explanation: The 5th second of A is the 3rd second of B. Distance in nth second = u + 1/2 a(2n-1). For A: 1/2 a₁ (2*5 - 1) = 9/2 a₁. For B: 1/2 a₂ (2*3 - 1) = 5/2 a₂. 9/2 a₁ = 5/2 a₂ ⇒ a₁ / a₂ = 5/9.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.text: A point traversed half of the distance with a velocity v₀. The remaining part with v₁ for half of the time and with the velocity v₂ for the other half of the time. The mean velocity of the point over the whole time of motion will be:
questions.explanation: For the second half of distance, average velocity v' = (v₁ + v₂) / 2. Now, average velocity for the entire distance with speeds v₀ and v' covering half distances each is <V> = 2 * v₀ * v' / (v₀ + v') = 2 v₀ ((v₁+v₂)/2) / (v₀ + (v₁+v₂)/2) = 2v₀(v₁+v₂) / (2v₀+v₁+v₂).

questions.subject: Physics
questions.text: A car moving with a speed of 40 km/hr can be stopped by applying brakes after at least 2 metres. If the same car is moving with a speed of 80 km/hr. Then the minimum stopping distance will be:
questions.explanation: Stopping distance s = v² / (2a). Since deceleration 'a' is constant, s ∝ v². When speed is doubled, stopping distance becomes 2² = 4 times. 4 * 2m = 8m.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.text: A body covers 200 cm in the first two seconds and 220 cm in the next 4 seconds. The velocity of the body at the end of the 7th second will be (assume acceleration to be constant):
questions.explanation: S₂ = 2u + 1/2 a(4) = 200 ⇒ u + a = 100. S₆ = 6u + 1/2 a(36) = 420 (total distance in 6s) ⇒ u + 3a = 70. Subtracting: 2a = -30 ⇒ a = -15 cm/s², u = 115 cm/s. Velocity at t=7: v = u + at = 115 + (-15)(7) = 115 - 105 = 10 cm/s.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.text: An elevator car whose floor to ceiling distance is 2.7 m starts ascending with a constant acceleration of 1.2 m/s². 2 seconds after the start a bolt falls from the ceiling of the car. The free fall time of the bolt is: (g = 9.8 m/s²)
questions.explanation: Relative acceleration of the bolt w.r.t the elevator is a_rel = g - (-a) = 9.8 + 1.2 = 11 m/s² downwards. Distance = 2.7 m. s = 1/2 a_rel t² ⇒ 2.7 = 1/2 (11) t² ⇒ t = √(5.4 / 11) s.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.text: Two trains, each of 50 m length are moving parallel on parallel lines towards each other with speeds of 10 m/s and 15 m/s. They pass each other in:
questions.explanation: Total distance to be covered = 50 + 50 = 100 m. Relative speed = 10 + 15 = 25 m/s (moving towards each other). Time = 100 / 25 = 4 s.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.text: The engineer of a train moving at a speed v₁ sights a freight train a distance d ahead of him on the same track moving in the same direction with a slow speed v₂. He puts on the brakes and gives his train a constant deceleration a. Then there will be no collision if:
questions.explanation: Relative velocity = v₁ - v₂. Relative deceleration = a. For no collision, the relative stopping distance must be less than d: (v₁ - v₂)² / (2a) < d, or d > (v₁ - v₂)² / (2a).

questions.subject: Physics
questions.text: A boat is moving with a velocity of 3î + 4ĵ and water in river is moving with a velocity -3î - 4ĵ with respect to ground. The relative velocity of boat with respect to water is:
questions.explanation: Relative velocity V_bw = V_b - V_w = (3î + 4ĵ) - (-3î - 4ĵ) = 6î + 8ĵ.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.text: A car accelerates from rest at a constant rate α for some time, after which it decelerates at a constant rate β and comes to rest. When total elapsed time is t, then the maximum velocity acquired by the car is:
questions.explanation: Max velocity v_max = α t₁ = β t₂. Total time t = t₁ + t₂ = v_max/α + v_max/β = v_max (1/α + 1/β) = v_max ((α+β)/(αβ)). Therefore, v_max = (αβ t) / (α+β).
